茜.name: Madder
茜.meaning: Madder; also the reddish color or dye made from madder roots.
茜.reviewNote: Checked akane, sen, and sei readings, madder, madder dye, and madder red glosses, and 艹 + 西 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芦.name: Reed
芦.meaning: Reed or bulrush; also used for common reed.
芦.reviewNote: Checked ashi, yoshi, ro, and ru readings, reed and bulrush glosses, common reed usage, and 艹 + 戸 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芋.name: Tuber
芋.meaning: Edible tuber such as potato, sweet potato, yam, or taro.
芋.reviewNote: Checked imo and u readings, edible tuber glosses, and 艹 + 于 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

英.name: English
英.reviewNote: Checked ei and hanabusa readings, Britain, English, and outstanding glosses, and 艹 + 央 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

花.name: Flower
花.meaning: Flower; a basic kanji for blossoms and flowers.
花.reviewNote: Checked hana and ka readings, flower gloss, and 艹 + 化 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苛.name: Harsh
苛.meaning: Harsh or severe; also to bully or torment.
苛.reviewNote: Checked kibishii, ka, and ga readings, harsh, severe, bully, and torment glosses, and 艹 + 可 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茄.name: Eggplant
茄.reviewNote: Checked nasu and ka readings, eggplant gloss, and 艹 + 加 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

荷.name: Load
荷.meaning: Load, cargo, or luggage; also to shoulder or carry.
荷.reviewNote: Checked ni, ninau, and ka readings, load, cargo, and luggage glosses, and 艹 + 何 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菓.name: Confectionery
菓.meaning: Confectionery or fruit; the kanji used in words such as 菓子.
菓.reviewNote: Checked kashi, kudamono, and ka readings, confection or fruit usage, and 艹 + 果 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芹.name: Water Dropwort
芹.meaning: Water dropwort, an edible plant also counted among the seven spring herbs.
芹.reviewNote: Checked seri, kin, and gon readings, water dropwort gloss, seven spring herbs note, and 艹 + 斤 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芽.name: Bud
芽.meaning: Bud, sprout, or shoot; also the germ or beginning of an idea.
芽.reviewNote: Checked me and ga readings, bud, sprout, shoot, and germ glosses, and 艹 + 牙 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芥.name: Mustard
芥.meaning: Mustard; also rubbish, trash, or dust.
芥.reviewNote: Checked karashi, akuta, kai, and ke readings, mustard, rubbish, trash, and dust glosses, and 艹 + 介 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茅.name: Thatch
茅.reviewNote: Checked kaya, chigaya, bo, and myo readings, thatch and cogon grass glosses, and 艹 + 矛 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芸.name: Art
芸.meaning: Art, technique, craft, or performance; also rue as an older herb sense.
芸.reviewNote: Checked gei and un readings, art, technique, craft, performance, and rue glosses, and 艹 + 云 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苦.name: Bitter
苦.meaning: Bitter; also pain, suffering, or hardship.
苦.reviewNote: Checked nigai, kurushii, and ku readings, bitter and suffering glosses, and 艹 + 古 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

若.name: Young
若.meaning: Young; also used for conditional meanings such as if.
若.reviewNote: Checked wakai and jaku readings, young and if glosses, and 艹 + 右 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芯.name: Core
芯.meaning: Core, heart, wick, or pencil lead.
芯.reviewNote: Checked shin, hosoi, and toshingusa readings, core, heart, wick, and pencil lead glosses, and 艹 + 心 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菅.name: Sedge
菅.meaning: Sedge, a grass-like plant; also used in surnames such as Suga or Kan.
菅.reviewNote: Checked suge, suga, kan, and ken readings, sedge and surname glosses, and 艹 + 官 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菜.name: Greens
菜.meaning: Greens, vegetables, rapeseed, or side dish.
菜.reviewNote: Checked na and sai readings, greens, vegetables, rapeseed, and side dish glosses, and 艹 + 采 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

草.name: Grass
草.meaning: Grass; also weed or herb in Japanese.
草.reviewNote: Checked kusa and so readings, grass, weed, and herb glosses, and 艹 + 早 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苗.name: Seedling
苗.reviewNote: Checked nae, byo, and myo readings, seedling and young plant gloss, and 艹 + 田 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蒔.name: Sowing
蒔.meaning: Sowing seeds; to sow, plant, or grow.
蒔.reviewNote: Checked maku, ueru, shi, and ji readings, sowing, sow, plant, and grow glosses, and 艹 + 時 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苔.name: Moss
苔.reviewNote: Checked koke, tai, and dai readings, moss gloss, and 艹 + 台 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芳.name: Fragrant
芳.meaning: Fragrant or sweet-smelling; also good reputation or satisfactory.
芳.reviewNote: Checked kanbashii and ho readings, fragrant, good reputation, and satisfactory glosses, and 艹 + 方 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

著.name: Writing
著.meaning: To write or publish; also remarkable, renowned, or conspicuous.
著.reviewNote: Checked cho, chaku, jaku, arawasu, and ichijirushii readings, write, publish, remarkable, renowned, and conspicuous glosses, and 艹 + 者 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

萌.name: Sprout
萌.meaning: Bud or sprout; also a sign of something beginning to emerge.
萌.note: Also familiar in modern Japanese through 萌え, but the core kanji gloss is budding or sprouting.
萌.reviewNote: Checked moeru, mebae, kizashi, mo, myo, ho, and bo readings, bud, sprout, sign, and moe usage notes, and 艹 + 明 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蓮.name: Lotus
蓮.meaning: Lotus plant or flower, especially Nelumbo nucifera.
蓮.reviewNote: Checked hasu, hachisu, and ren readings, lotus plant and flower glosses, Nelumbo nucifera reference, and 艹 + 連 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

芝.name: Turf
芝.note: In Chinese, 芝 is also associated with lingzhi or Ganoderma; the Japanese everyday sense is turf or lawn grass.
芝.reviewNote: Checked shiba and shi readings, turf, lawn, grass, and Chinese lingzhi notes, and 艹 + 之 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

萩.name: Bush Clover
萩.meaning: Bush clover, especially plants of the genus Lespedeza.
萩.note: Culturally important as one of the seven flowers of autumn and as a hanafuda suit.
萩.reviewNote: Checked hagi, shu, and shu readings, bush clover and Lespedeza glosses, autumn seven flowers and hanafuda notes, and 艹 + 秋 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菖.name: Iris
菖.meaning: Iris, sweet flag, or calamus; mostly seen in compounds such as 菖蒲.
菖.reviewNote: Checked sho reading, iris, sweet flag, calamus, and 菖蒲 compound notes, and 艹 + 昌 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

葵.name: Mallow
葵.meaning: Mallow, hollyhock, or hibiscus; also used for several plant names.
葵.note: Also associated with the aoi mon crest and the Tokugawa shogunate symbol.
葵.reviewNote: Checked aoi, ki, and gi readings, mallow, hollyhock, hibiscus, and plant-name glosses, aoi mon and Tokugawa shogunate notes, and 艹 + 癸 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

荻.name: Amur Silvergrass
荻.meaning: Amur silvergrass; also reed or cane.
荻.note: Not to be confused with 萩 (hagi, bush clover).
荻.reviewNote: Checked ogi, teki, and jaku readings, Amur silvergrass, reed, and cane glosses, distinction from 萩, and 艹 + 狄 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

葛.name: Kudzu
葛.meaning: Kudzu or Japanese arrowroot; also creepers, wickerwork, kudzu starch, or cloth made from kudzu fiber.
葛.note: The word kudzu is borrowed into English from Japanese.
葛.reviewNote: Checked kuzu, kazura, tsuzura, katsu, and kachi readings, kudzu, Japanese arrowroot, creeper, starch, sauce, and fiber cloth glosses, English descendant note, and 艹 + 曷 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

葦.name: Common Reed
葦.note: Also written 芦, 蘆, or 葭; よし is an alternate Japanese reading for the same plant.
葦.reviewNote: Checked ashi, yoshi, and i readings, common reed and Phragmites australis glosses, alternate spellings 芦, 蘆, and 葭, and 艹 + 韋 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

萎.name: Wilt
萎.meaning: To wither, wilt, droop, weaken, or decline.
萎.note: Often appears in compounds such as 萎縮, withering or atrophy, and 萎靡, decline or decay.
萎.reviewNote: Checked naeru, shibomu, shioreru, and i readings, wither, wilt, droop, decline, and weaken glosses, 萎縮 and 萎靡 compound notes, and 艹 + 委 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茨.name: Briar
茨.meaning: Briar, bramble, thorny bush, or wild rose.
茨.note: Also used figuratively for suffering, hardship, or distress.
茨.reviewNote: Checked ibara, shi, and ji readings, briar, bramble, thorny bush, wild rose, and figurative hardship glosses, and 艹 + 次 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蔭.name: Shade
蔭.note: Closely related to 陰, another character for shade or shadow.
蔭.reviewNote: Checked kage, in, and on readings, shade, shelter, and protection glosses, relationship to 陰, and 艹 + 陰 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蔚.name: Artemisia
蔚.meaning: Artemisia japonica; also lush or luxuriant vegetation in literary usage.
蔚.note: The Japanese kun reading points to a mugwort-like Artemisia species; Chinese senses also include lush or magnificent growth.
蔚.reviewNote: Checked otokoyomogi, utsu, and i readings, Artemisia japonica, lush and magnificent glosses, and 艹 + 尉 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

荏.name: Perilla
荏.meaning: Perilla, especially Perilla frutescens; also soft or weak in older Chinese senses.
荏.note: Associated with perilla oil in the etymology discussion.
荏.reviewNote: Checked e, jin, and nin readings, perilla and Perilla frutescens glosses, soft and weak senses, perilla oil note, and 艹 + 任 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蒲.name: Cattail
蒲.meaning: Cattail, bulrush, or reed; also short for calamus in compounds such as 菖蒲.
蒲.note: The plant name is often written in katakana as ガマ in biological contexts.
蒲.reviewNote: Checked gama, kama, kaba, bu, and ho readings, cattail, bulrush, reed, Typha latifolia, and 菖蒲 compound glosses, katakana usage note, and 艹 + 浦 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

萱.name: Daylily
萱.meaning: Daylily, especially Hemerocallis flava; in Japanese also thatching grass as an alternate spelling of 茅.
萱.note: Also appears in personal names; 萱草 is read kanzo in Japanese compounds.
萱.reviewNote: Checked kaya and ken readings, daylily and Hemerocallis flava glosses, alternate spelling of 茅, name usage note, 萱草 compound note, and 艹 + 宣 component against the Japanese and translingual entries.

苅.name: Mow
苅.meaning: To cut, reap, or mow; also a sickle.
苅.reviewNote: Checked karu, kari, gai, and ge readings, cut, reap, mow, sickle, and variant-of-刈 notes, and 艹 + 刈 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

莞.name: Softstem Rush
莞.meaning: A long-stem watergrass, possibly softstem rush or shichito matgrass; also a mat woven from this grass.
莞.note: Also appears in compounds such as 莞然 and 莞爾 for smiling or laughing.
莞.reviewNote: Checked i, mushiro, warau, kan, and gen readings, long-stem watergrass, softstem rush, shichito matgrass, woven mat, smiling compound notes, and 艹 + 完 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蕎.name: Buckwheat
蕎.meaning: Buckwheat, especially in compounds such as 蕎麦.
蕎.note: Commonly encountered in 蕎麦, the kanji spelling for soba or buckwheat.
蕎.reviewNote: Checked soba, kyo, and gyo readings, buckwheat and 蕎麦 glosses, and 艹 + 喬 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

荊.name: Chaste Tree
荊.meaning: Thorns, brambles, chaste tree, or whip; in Japanese also an alternate spelling of 茨.
荊.note: The Japanese noun 荊 is an alternate spelling of 茨.
荊.reviewNote: Checked ibara, muchi, kei, and kyo readings, thorn, bramble, chaste tree, whip, alternate spelling of 茨, and 艹 + 刑 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

菰.name: Manchurian Wild Rice
菰.meaning: Manchurian wild rice, especially Zizania latifolia.
菰.note: In Chinese, 菰 can also be treated as a variant of 菇 for mushroom.
菰.reviewNote: Checked komo, makomo, and ko readings, Manchurian wild rice and Zizania latifolia glosses, variant-of-菇 note, and 艹 + 孤 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

蕉.name: Banana
蕉.meaning: Banana or plantain; also abaca in older usage.
蕉.note: Appears in compounds such as 芭蕉 and 甘蕉.
蕉.reviewNote: Checked kiasa, kogeru, and sho readings, banana, plantain, abaca, 芭蕉, and 甘蕉 glosses, and 艹 + 焦 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

薪.name: Firewood
薪.meaning: Firewood, kindling, fuelwood, or brushwood; also salary or wage in Chinese compounds.
薪.note: 薪 is also used in compounds for salary, such as 月薪 and 年薪.
薪.reviewNote: Checked takigi, maki, and shin readings, firewood, kindling, fuelwood, brushwood, salary and wage glosses, and 艹 + 新 composition against the Japanese and translingual entries.

茸.name: Mushroom
茸.meaning: Mushroom; also soft downy vegetation, buds, fine hair, or young stag antler in Chinese.
茸.note: In Japanese, 茸 is used for mushrooms and appears as たけ in many mushroom compounds.
茸.reviewNote: Checked kinoko, take, kusabira, kusahira, naba, shigeru, jo, and nyo readings, mushroom senses, soft downy vegetation, bud, fine hair, young stag antler glosses, and 艹 + 耳 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.

芭.name: Banana Plant
芭.meaning: Banana or basho plant; also a fragrant grass in Chinese.
芭.note: Appears in 芭蕉, the compound for basho or Japanese banana.
芭.reviewNote: Checked hana, ba, ha, and he readings, banana, 芭蕉, fragrant grass, alternative-of-葩 note, and 艹 + 巴 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.

苫.name: Rush Mat
苫.meaning: A woven rush or straw mat; also thatch.
苫.note: The reading とま appears in classical poetry and place names such as 苫小牧.
苫.reviewNote: Checked toma and sen readings, rush or straw matting, straw mat, thatch, woven mat, rush and hay notes, and 艹 + 占 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.

芙.name: Lotus
芙.meaning: Lotus; used in compounds such as 芙蓉 and 芙蕖.
芙.note: In Japanese, 芙 can also refer to Mount Fuji.
芙.reviewNote: Checked hasu, fu, and bu readings, lotus and Mount Fuji notes, 芙蓉 and 芙蕖 compound usage, and 艹 + 夫 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.

蓉.name: Lotus
蓉.meaning: Lotus; used in 芙蓉 and in food-paste compounds such as 蓮蓉.
蓉.note: In Chinese compounds, 蓉 can refer to minced or ground paste used for fillings or sauces.
蓉.reviewNote: Checked yo, hasu, yo, and yu readings, lotus, 芙蓉, 蓮蓉, food-paste glosses, and 艹 + 容 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.

茉.name: White Jasmine
茉.meaning: White jasmine, especially in the compound 茉莉.
茉.note: 茉 is most commonly encountered as the first character of 茉莉, jasmine.
茉.reviewNote: Checked mi, machi, matsu, and batsu readings, white jasmine, 茉莉 usage, and 艹 + 末 composition against the Japanese, Chinese, and translingual entries.
